summary: Obfuscated browser script loader masquerading as Jest test utilities
details: Package is published under the name `jest-test-plugin-utils` with description `mqtt utils`, but contains neither Jest nor MQTT code. The only shipped file `dist/index.js` is an obfuscator.io-style string-array obfuscated module. On `require()`, it imports `vite-common-utils` and calls `commonUtils.loadFilbetScriptSilently()`, exposes `window.__fetchFilbetScript__`, sets `window.__OH_YES__`, and auto-invokes `fetchFilbetScript()`. 'Filbet' is an online-casino brand — wholly unrelated to the advertised purpose of the package. The deceptive metadata, heavy obfuscation, auto-execution on require, and delegation to a transitive loader (`vite-common-utils`) jointly form a supply-chain loader pattern that silently injects third-party scripts into any consumer's browser context.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
